title: India Considers Boosting Imports of U.S. Oil and Coal
summary: India should increase the amount of crude oil and coking coal it imports from the United States as a way to diversify its supply under the interim trade deal between New Delhi and Washington, according to the country’s commerce and industry minister. title: Guyana Emerges as the Clear Winner in Venezuela’s Oil Reset
summary: One of the biggest winners from the new oil order in Venezuela is its neighbor to the east, Guyana, the country that turned into the newest oil producer in the world thanks to ExxonMobil’s massive oil discoveries. title: U.S. Crude Oil Inventories Plunge Following Massive Build
summary: The American Petroleum Institute (API) estimated that crude oil inventories in the United States fell by 609,000 barrels in the week ending February 13, after increasing by 13.4 million barrels in the week prior. Inventories in the US Strategic Petroleum Reserve (SPR) keep climbing week after week.…

title: Eni plots return to oil and gas trading as it eyes rivals’ profits
